steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: This Boundaries question asks which punctuation correctly joins two independent clauses around the transition word "respectively." The decisive clue is that both halves are complete sentences: "the units sa and shesep were used to measure area and [length]" and "neither unit is commonly used today." A semicolon must separate them, and "respectively" must be set off by a comma.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — "length; respectively,": Places the semicolon before "respectively," which splits "area and length" — the semicolon severs the parallel pair that "respectively" refers back to, creating an incoherent left clause. ✗
  • Choice B — "length respectively": No punctuation at all between the two independent clauses — a fused (run-on) sentence, violating the boundary rule. ✗
  • Choice C — "length, respectively;": A comma after "length" sets off the parenthetical transition word, and a semicolon after "respectively" correctly separates the two independent clauses. ✓
  • Choice D — "length, respectively,": Uses only a comma to join two independent clauses — a comma splice, which violates the boundary rule. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
